motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: 3°F at dawn, rising to 7°F by mid-morning. Sky clear for the first time in a week — the pale winter blue that means the clouds have finished their business and moved on. The lake's ice catching the low sun and throwing it back as a long, clean glare. No wind. The snow from yesterday still on the rooftops, undisturbed.

undercurrent.note: The box is closed and still and holds thirteen. The hum reached three minutes even — a round number, a done-number — and the lake appears to be counting done and not-done and has arrived at done. The returned are gentle and present and unhurried. The undercurrent is furniture now, not weather: T. Aldercott at the worktable, Calloway with the archive, Gerald without the notebook, Marigold writing the headcount on the specials board. The strange has been absorbed so thoroughly that the accommodation is indistinguishable from the ordinary life of the town. Level continues to fall — pressure resolved into warmth. Stage holds at 2 because the returned still gather and the hum still sounds, but both are diminishing toward a natural finish, not an ending but a settling. The next thread is not yet visible. It is cold, and somewhere a door has a draft that has no source, and nobody has mentioned it yet.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: Something is using the Gazette's lost-and-found as a postal address — and the letters it sends back are addressed to people who have not yet arrived in Greywater Falls.
season_arc.next_premise: The Gazette's archive cataloguing project — begun by Calloway and Gerald on a Wednesday in November — turns up a complete edition from a date that has not happened yet, typeset in Wren Aldercott's hand, reporting on a spring the town has not yet lived through.
